questions.q:
Paragraph: A titration curve graph of pH as a function of the volume of titrant, displays in detail how the pH changes over the course of an acid-base titration. Significantly, the pH changes most rapidly near the equivalence point. The exact shape of a titration curve depends on the relative strengths of acids and bases. Examine the titration curve below:
Question: The suitable indicator for this titration will be
questions.solution: pH range of all these indicators lies in between 2-12. Hence, all are suitable indicators.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Redox reactions play a vital role in chemistry and biology. The values of $\mathrm{E}_{\text {rol }}^{\circ}$ of two half cell reactions decide which way the reaction is expected to proceed. A simple example is Daniel cell in which zinc goes into solution and copper gets deposited. Given below are a set of half-cell reactions (acidic medium) along with their $\mathrm{E}^0$ values. $\begin{array}{ll}\mathrm{I}_2+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}^{-} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.54 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Cl}_2+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.36 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Mn}^{3+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.50 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.77 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{O}_2+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.23 \mathrm{~V}\end{array}$Question: Among the following identify the correct statement:
options: lodide ion is oxidized by chlorine
questions.solution: Reduction potential of $\mathrm{I}_2$ is less than $\mathrm{Cl}_2$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

Significantly, the pH changes most rapidly near the equivalence point. The exact shape of a titration curve depends on the relative strengths of acids and bases. Examine the titration curve below:
Question: The pH at equivalence point is
questions.solution: Since curve is given for strong acid and strong base hence, pH at the end point is 7 .

questions.q: Paragraph: Redox reactions play a vital role in chemistry and biology. The values of $\mathrm{E}_{\text {rol }}^{\circ}$ of two half cell reactions decide which way the reaction is expected to proceed. A simple example is Daniel cell in which zinc goes into solution and copper gets deposited. Given below are a set of half-cell reactions (acidic medium) along with their $\mathrm{E}^0$ values. $\begin{array}{ll}\mathrm{I}_2+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{I}^{-} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.54 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Cl}_2+2 \mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cl}^{-} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.36 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Mn}^{3+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Mn}^{2+} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.50 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{Fe}^{3+}+\mathrm{e}^{-} \longrightarrow \mathrm{Fe}^{2+} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=0.77 \mathrm{~V} \\ \mathrm{O}_2+4 \mathrm{H}^{+}+4 \mathrm{e}^{-} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O} ; & \mathrm{E}^{\circ}=1.23 \mathrm{~V}\end{array}$Question: $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ is stable but $\mathrm{Mn}^{3 *}$ is not stable in acidic solution because:
options: $\mathrm{O}_2$ oxidises both $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ into $\mathrm{Mn}^{3+}$ and $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ respectively.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
